title: curio articulatus
intro: Curio articulatus, also known as Groundsel bush, is a shrubby plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its small yellow flowers and pinnate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems. The essential oils extracted from Curio articulatus have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
